Open Access status of publications in time — tracking how open-access publishing practices have evolved over time

title: Open Access status of publications in time
text:
Open Access status of publications in time
As an ex-researcher and ex-consultant in the field of Higher Education and Research, I have been interested for a long time in publications practices.

title: Open Access status in time
text:
# libraries and imports
import sys
import requests
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
plt.style.use('../matplotlib.mplstyle')

In this study, we will get data from OpenAlex to extract data on how the Open Access status of publications has evolved in the past 10 years.

# first, we define a handy function that will help us perform queries to the OpenAlex APIs.
def send_query(url, params, print_query=False):

        # perform the request using the specified URL and parameters
        r = requests.get(url, params)

        if print_query:
            print(r.request.url)

        # check the status code of the request
        if r.status_code != 200:

            if r.status_code == 414:
                raise ValueError("""API error: {}""".format(r.reason))
            else:
                import ast
                err = ast.literal_eval(r.text)
                raise ValueError("""API error:\n\tERROR  :  {error}\n\tMESSAGE:  {message}""".format(**err))
        else:

            # if there is no error return the JSON object for the results
            return r.json()


# then, we obtain the data, starting from MIN_YEAR and ending at MAX_YEAR, both included
MIN_YEAR = 2015
MAX_YEAR = 2025
OA_API_URL = 'https://api.openalex.org'
oa_status_year = {}
for year in range(MIN_YEAR, MAX_YEAR+1):
    # build the URL for the request
    URL = f'{OA_API_URL}/works'
    tmp = send_query(
        URL,
        params={'filter':f'type:article,publication_year:{year}', 'group_by':'oa_status'})['group_by']
    oa_status_year[year] = pd.DataFrame(tmp).set_index('key')['count'].rename('npapers')


oa_status_year = pd.DataFrame(oa_status_year)
oa_status_year.sum()

2015    7207568
2016    7246654
2017    6853936
2018    6810353
2019    7087018
2020    7227151
2021    6644701
2022    6043447
2023    6322328
2024    6655393
2025    7691611
dtype: int64



# prepare data
plot_df = (oa_status_year/1e6).T
order_status = plot_df.sum(axis=0).sort_values(ascending=False).index
plot_df = plot_df[order_status]

# init figure
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7,3.5))

# color mapping
colors = {
    'closed':'red',
    'gold':'xkcd:gold',
    'bronze':'xkcd:bronze',
    'green':'green',
    'hybrid':'xkcd:light blue',
    'diamond':'xkcd:emerald'
}

for col in plot_df.columns:
    plot_df[col].plot(
        color=colors[col],
        linewidth=3,
        label=col.capitalize(),
        ax=ax
    )

ax.set_xlabel('Year')
ax.set_ylabel('Number of publications (M)')

# legend
leg = ax.legend(title='', ncols=6, loc='lower left', bbox_to_anchor=(0,1), handlelength=0.3, columnspacing=0.8)
for legobj in leg.legend_handles:
    legobj.set_linewidth(7.0)

ax.set_title('Evolution of number of publications per year by Open Access status', loc='left', pad=30, fontsize=12, fontweight='bold')
ax.grid(axis='y', linestyle='--', linewidth=0.5)
ax.text(2014.5, -1., "Source: OpenAlex\nData visualization by Ruggero Cortini", color='xkcd:dark grey',fontsize=8)

fig.savefig('n_year.png', dpi=600, bbox_inches='tight')

title: Curriculum Vitae
text:
Summary

Data scientist and consultant with a PhD in biophysics and experience spanning academic research, management consulting, and applied ML engineering. Specialised in translating complex scientific and business problems into robust data solutions for pharma and biotech organisations.

title: About
text:
Background

I am a data scientist and consultant with a background in computational biophysics. After completing my PhD and several years in academic research, I transitioned into management consulting — working with higher education and research institutions on data strategy and analytics. I now focus on applied machine learning, AI systems, and data engineering for pharma and biotech clients.

CV content — fill in Experience and Education sections in cv.qmd
CV PDF — export your CV as PDF, save to assets/cv.pdf
About narrative — replace placeholder text in about.qmd with your own bio
Home tagline — review and personalise the one-liner in index.qmd
oa_time.qmd content — the project card renders but the page body is mostly placeholder; add the actual analysis





Projects — add more *.qmd files to projects/ (each needs title, date, description, categories in its YAML front matter to show as a card)
Photography — add images to assets/photography/, then uncomment and populate the gallery template in personal/photography/index.qmd
Fantasy maps — add images to assets/maps/, then uncomment and populate personal/maps/index.qmd

Verify quarto render completes without errors after the restructure
Push docs/ to GitHub Pages







Theme: cosmo — clean, Bootstrap 5 base, recruiter-readable; brand layer removed (no _brand.yml existed)
Projects listing: auto-generated via Quarto listing, grid layout sorted by date; add front matter to any .qmd in projects/ to get a card
Photography: uses Quarto’s built-in lightbox: true — no extension required
CV: styled HTML page at cv.qmd + download button pointing to assets/cv.pdf
Personal pages: one dropdown click deeper from nav — visible but not leading
